title: Today's AI Brief: AirTrunk commits $30B to build 5GW of AI data centers in India
summary: A $30 billion investment in AI data centers in India and the impact of AI on font design.
imageAlt: Image from source article for AirTrunk commits $30B to build 5GW of AI data centers in India
imageCaption: AirTrunk commits $30B to build 5GW of AI data centers in India
imageSourceTitle: AirTrunk commits $30B to build 5GW of AI data centers in India

body.text: I'm checking out a couple of interesting AI-related news items. First up, AirTrunk, a Blackstone-backed data center operator, is making a massive $30 billion investment in India by 2030 to build 5GW of AI data centers. This is part of a larger trend of tech and infrastructure companies expanding their computing capacity in the region. It's a pretty big deal, and I'm curious to see how it plays out.

So, why India? The country's growing tech industry and government support for data center development make it an attractive location. AirTrunk's massive investment will likely create new opportunities for AI adoption and innovation in the region. It's like the AI gold rush, but instead of panning for gold, they're building data centers. I'm guessing we'll be hearing a lot more about this kind of investment in the coming months.

In other news, AI is shaking up the world of font design. According to Wired, serif fonts are falling out of favor, and AI-generated fonts are taking their place. I mean, who needs serifs when you can have AI-created fonts that are, allegedly, more efficient and scalable? It's an interesting example of how AI is influencing design choices, even if it's not immediately clear what the long-term impact will be.

AirTrunk commits $30B to build 5GW of AI data centers in India is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Blackstone-backed data center operator AirTrunk said on Friday it would invest $30 billion in India by 2030, adding to a wave of commitments from technology and infrastructure groups seeking to expand computing capacity in the country. The Australian company said it would develop 5 gigawatts of new data center capacity in India, one of the largest commitmen... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

AI Has Come for Serif Fonts is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: As public backlash to the seeming omnipresence of artificial intelligence intensifies, the collective quest to weed out—and reject —telltale signs of its use continues. One of the first casualties, to my dismay, was em dashes—which are a great, and very human form of punctuation, by the way! There's also the “rule of threes,” which is meant to scan as rhyth... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
